# @zakkster/lite-headless / anchor

Sidebar scrollspy. As the user scrolls through a long document, the
sidebar link pointing at the section currently in view is marked
active. Common in docs sites, long-form articles, multi-section
dashboards.

## How it works

One `IntersectionObserver` watches all linked sections. When a
section's intersection ratio crosses, the "earliest visible section"
(in DOM order) wins and its corresponding link is marked active.

A negative top-margin equal to `offsetTop` shifts the activation
trigger down, so a fixed header doesn't fight with the active-link
calculation.

Click handlers smoothly scroll to the target and optimistically
mark the link active without waiting for IO to catch up.

## Quick example

```js
import { createAnchor } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/anchor";

const a = createAnchor({
    offsetTop: 64,        // shift activation down by header height
    smooth: true,         // smooth-scroll on link click
    onChange: (key) => {},
});

a.attachRoot(sidebarEl);
a.attachLink(introLink, introSection, "intro");
a.attachLink(installLink, installSection, "install");
a.attachLink(apiLink, apiSection, "api");
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `root` | `null` (viewport) | Scroll container; passed to IntersectionObserver |
| `offsetTop` | `0` | px; shifts activation trigger down (account for fixed header) |
| `smooth` | `true` | Smooth-scroll on link click |
| `onChange` | - | `(key: string \| null) => void` |

## API

```ts
{
    activeKey(): string | null
    linkCount: number   // readonly

    attachRoot(linkContainerEl): off
    attachLink(linkEl, sectionEl, key?: string): off

    destroy(): void
}
```

If `key` is omitted, the section's `id` is used. If both are absent,
a synthetic key is generated.

## Painted attributes

### Root (`[data-anchor-root]`)

(slot marker only)

### Links (`[data-anchor-link]`)

- `data-active` ‚Äî boolean, true for the currently-active link
- `aria-current="location"` ‚Äî set on the active link, removed otherwise

### Sections (`[data-anchor-section]`)

- `data-anchor-section-key="<key>"` ‚Äî the identifier used for matching

## Wrapper (`<lite-anchor>`)

```html
<lite-anchor offset-top="64">
    <a href="#intro">Intro</a>
    <a href="#install">Install</a>
    <a href="#api">API</a>
</lite-anchor>

<!-- ...elsewhere on the page... -->
<section id="intro">...</section>
<section id="install">...</section>
<section id="api">...</section>
```

Auto-discovery: any `<a href="#...">` or any element with
`data-anchor-target="#..."` inside `<lite-anchor>` is wired. The
matching section is found by `document.querySelector` on the hash.

Attributes:
- `offset-top` ‚Äî px (default 0)
- `smooth` ‚Äî set `"false"` for instant jump
- `root` ‚Äî CSS selector for scroll container

Event: `activechange` ‚Äî `{ detail: { key } }`

## CSS contract

```css
[data-anchor-link] {
    display: block;
    padding: 6px 12px;
    color: var(--fg-dim);
    text-decoration: none;
    border-left: 2px solid transparent;
}
[data-anchor-link][data-active] {
    color: var(--accent);
    border-left-color: var(--accent);
    background: rgba(125,211,252,0.06);
}
```
